@@ -275,7 +275,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-ih4tm","title":"[MEDIUM] defer_fts_triggers=false is not reversible: triggers stay dropped after enabling once (ft-wk5fo follow-up)","description":"# Defect

## Concrete failure path

File: `crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs`.

d74401c6 landed `StorageConfig.defer_fts_triggers` (ft-wk5fo). The intent is a reversible opt-in: flip to `true` to trade FTS freshness for write-path latency, flip back to `false` to restore synchronous indexing. The inline comment at line 6006 says as much:

> The triggers can be re-created by re-opening with `defer_fts_triggers: false`

That claim is wrong. Concrete interleaving:

1. Operator opens DB with `defer_fts_triggers: true` on an up-to-date schema (`user_version == SCHEMA_VERSION`).
2. `initialize_schema` returns early at line 4315 (no SQL executed, up-to-date path).
3. The explicit `DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS output_segments_ai/ad/au` at line 6010 runs — triggers gone.
4. Operator flips config to `defer_fts_triggers: false` and restarts.
5. `initialize_schema` again short-circuits at line 4315.
6. Line 6010 is skipped (flag is false). **No `CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS` runs.**
7. Database silently stays in deferred mode; the operator's `false` setting is a no-op.

The inline comment at line 6007 hints the author knew: 'this commit ships the opt-in direction only; reverting would require re-running SCHEMA_SQL or the operator dropping the DB.' That matches reality but contradicts the line-6006 'can be re-created by re-opening' claim — and leaves the config behaving non-intuitively for operators.

## Severity rationale

- Not a crash or data loss.
- Silent wrong answer: operator flips back to `false`, expects synchronous FTS, gets deferred.
- One-way-door config is a known anti-pattern. Easy fix.

## Fix sketch

Make the open path truly bidirectional. On every open, after `initialize_schema` returns:

* If `defer_fts_triggers == true`: `DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS ...` (idempotent).
* If `defer_fts_triggers == false`: `CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS ...` using the same SQL the SCHEMA_SQL block defines.

Factor the trigger SQL into one private const referenced by both branches so SCHEMA_SQL and the re-create branch cannot drift.

## Tests

* ft_ih4tm_defer_fts_triggers_roundtrip — open with true, verify triggers dropped, close, reopen with false, verify triggers re-created.
* ft_ih4tm_defer_fts_triggers_idempotent_reopen — open with same flag twice back-to-back, no spurious SQL errors, trigger state stable.

## Scope

Single file (`storage.rs`). No API break (`defer_fts_triggers` already public, default `false`).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:27:12.433144Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:31:50.095316Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:31:50.095017Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-ii8ss","title":"[security] wa.get_text 'tail' field has no upper bound — user-controlled usize allows unbounded scrollback fetch (memory-pressure vector); apply the LIMIT_MIN/LIMIT_MAX pattern from wa.events","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-26T06:39:39.458126Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T07:21:39.223347Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T07:21:39.222765Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-ismux","title":"[mission][dispatch] MissionDispatcher reports accepted dispatch without calling WorkflowRunner::handle_detection","description":"In `crates/frankenterm-core/src/mission_dispatch.rs:200-226`, `dispatch_single()` builds a synthetic detection, then only calls `runner.find_matching_workflow(&detection)` and fabricates `accepted: true` plus a synthetic `execution_id` when a workflow exists. It never invokes `WorkflowRunner::handle_detection()`, so no lock acquisition, no concurrency-limit enforcement, no storage row, and no real workflow start happen.

Impact: callers receive a success-shaped `DispatchResult` that claims work was accepted even though the runner was never asked to start anything. This also makes the existing close reason on `ft-y9lnb.3` overstated relative to the current code.

Fix direction: bridge the sync mission loop to the async runner start path with a bounded runtime, map the actual `WorkflowStartResult` into `DispatchResult`, and only emit started metadata from a real start result.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:05:13.673841Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:13:49.575203Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:13:49.574685Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of ft-4r2ib. crates/frankenterm-core/src/mission_dispatch.rs is already dirty in this checkout with the exact handle_detection/WorkflowStartResult fix and regression coverage, so I did not collide with pane-4-owned WIP.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"ft-iwlsh","title":"[perf][scan-pipeline] scan_locate allocates a per-call Vec on the chunked hot path; the caller never needs it","description":"Audit-pass profiling finding (AmberFox 2026-04-26). scan_pipeline.rs:394 is the ONLY production caller of TriggerScanner::scan_locate (the other 4 callers are inside #[cfg(test)] blocks). The call shape is:

  for matched in self.trigger_scanner.scan_locate(scratch) {
      if matched.offset.saturating_add(matched.length) <= overlap_len {
          continue;
      }
      ...
  }

scan_locate (pattern_trigger.rs:589-595) is a thin wrapper that allocates a fresh Vec::new() and then calls for_each_leftmost_match to push matches into it; the caller iterates and consumes the vec. The Vec is never retained. For high-match-rate inputs (compiler output with thousands of warnings, log streams full of ERROR markers) this is O(matches) allocation traffic on the same hot path ft-6db1t addressed for the trigger-counts HashMap.

Fix: rewrite scan_pipeline.rs:394 to use for_each_leftmost_match directly with a closure that performs the offset filter + array-indexed increment. Eliminates the per-call Vec allocation entirely. The wrapper scan_locate stays for the 4 test callers + external API.

Same proven pattern as ft-6db1t (TriggerCategoryCounts replacing HashMap<TriggerCategory, u64>): closure-driven hot path, allocation kept in the test-only convenience method.

Estimated impact: per-chunk allocation eliminated. On high-frequency capture paths (200+-pane fleets, log-heavy panes) this is amortized but non-trivial.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T22:44:01.861265Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T22:44:09.792376Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-iwlsh","title":"[perf][scan-pipeline] scan_locate allocates a per-call Vec on the chunked hot path; the caller never needs it","description":"Audit-pass profiling finding (AmberFox 2026-04-26). scan_pipeline.rs:394 is the ONLY production caller of TriggerScanner::scan_locate (the other 4 callers are inside #[cfg(test)] blocks). The call shape is:

  for matched in self.trigger_scanner.scan_locate(scratch) {
      if matched.offset.saturating_add(matched.length) <= overlap_len {
          continue;
      }
      ...
  }

scan_locate (pattern_trigger.rs:589-595) is a thin wrapper that allocates a fresh Vec::new() and then calls for_each_leftmost_match to push matches into it; the caller iterates and consumes the vec. The Vec is never retained. For high-match-rate inputs (compiler output with thousands of warnings, log streams full of ERROR markers) this is O(matches) allocation traffic on the same hot path ft-6db1t addressed for the trigger-counts HashMap.

Fix: rewrite scan_pipeline.rs:394 to use for_each_leftmost_match directly with a closure that performs the offset filter + array-indexed increment. Eliminates the per-call Vec allocation entirely. The wrapper scan_locate stays for the 4 test callers + external API.

Same proven pattern as ft-6db1t (TriggerCategoryCounts replacing HashMap<TriggerCategory, u64>): closure-driven hot path, allocation kept in the test-only convenience method.

Estimated impact: per-chunk allocation eliminated. On high-frequency capture paths (200+-pane fleets, log-heavy panes) this is amortized but non-trivial.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T22:44:01.861265Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T23:07:47.133725Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T23:07:47.133244Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-j1qjt.1","title":"[ft-j1qjt.1] extract frankenterm-core-capture-types leaf crate (replay_capture + FtreplayArtifact types core depends on)","description":"Per docs/proposals/ft-j1qjt-replay-tier1-blocker.md: extract the 7 types core's policy/runtime/workflows currently reach into the replay cluster for (replay_capture::SharedCaptureAdapter, DecisionEvent, DecisionType, CollectingCaptureSink, CaptureAdapter, CaptureConfig + replay_fixture_harvest::FtreplayArtifact) into a leaf crate following the frankenterm-core-resource-types (ft-usvnt) pattern. Re-export from core under crate::replay_capture::* + crate::replay_fixture_harvest::* so the 20 inline call sites keep resolving. Prerequisite for ft-j1qjt.2.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-26T05:38:09.436288Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T05:54:21.116179Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T05:54:21.115709Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","refactor","replay"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-j1qjt.1","depends_on_id":"ft-j1qjt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-26T05:38:09.436288Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Same template as the ars extraction (commit 876537fc). Empty crates/frankenterm-core-replay/ scaffold from this commit can either be reused or removed and recreated.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-26T05:38:09.687006Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T06:42:41.038412Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T06:42:41.037916Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","refactor","replay"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-j1qjt.2","depends_on_id":"ft-j1qjt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-26T05:38:09.687006Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -458,7 +458,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-wajba","title":"tmux cmd_queue drop-head-in-flight corrupts Guarded response routing","description":"frankenterm/mux/src/tmux.rs:73-91 (push_command_capped) vs :165-186 (Guarded response handler).

push_command_capped(queue, cmd) does queue.drain(..excess) when queue exceeds CMD_QUEUE_MAX_DEPTH=50_000. The drain is from the FRONT. But when TmuxDomainState.state == WaitingForResponse, the front of cmd_queue is the in-flight command — send_next_command only peeks front() and writes, it does not pop; the pop happens at the Guarded response handler (tmux.rs:172) via cmd_queue.pop_front().

Race / correctness gap: if push_command_capped runs while state == WaitingForResponse and queue.len() reaches 50_001, drain drops the in-flight head. When the Guarded response arrives, pop_front() pops a DIFFERENT pending command and calls cmd.process_result(domain_id, &response) on the wrong command — cross-wiring response payload onto the wrong command parser. Both threads hold the cmd_queue lock so the drain+pop ordering is deterministic under the lock, but the correctness violation stands.

Fix options (not applied here, pattern-level):
 1. When state == WaitingForResponse, skip index 0 in drain: queue.drain(1..=excess) (but preserves head).
 2. Drain from the back (newest-first) which discards most recently enqueued layout/session events that often re-fire.
 3. Abort the in-flight state machine (set state=Idle) and drain — heavy-handed but restores invariant.

Only triggers in pathological event storms (remote tmux lag + 50_000 queued commands) so severity is MEDIUM. The original bug fixed in 1055cffc (push-site cap enforcement) traded unbounded memory for this subtle correctness edge. A regression test would drive tmux into WaitingForResponse, push >50_001 Layout/SessionChanged events, then fire a Guarded response and assert process_result is NOT called against a different command type than the one that was sent.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:16:32.972876Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:34:41.760204Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:34:41.759836Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 9cc7e6c2. push_command_capped migrated from free function to TmuxDomainState method so it can consult self.state before choosing drain range:
- state == WaitingForResponse → drain(1..=excess) (preserves index 0, the in-flight cmd awaiting its Guarded response)
- any other state → drain(..excess) (retains existing drop-oldest semantics)

Both drain branches log distinctly so event-storm incidents are traceable. Lock ordering matches Guarded-handler's (cmd_queue first, state transiently inside method): no deadlock. send_next_command's cap-drain is untouched — already guarded by early 'state != Idle → return'.

Regression coverage: 2 new tests under mux::tmux::tests that each seat a distinguishable SplitPane at index 0 and push CMD_QUEUE_MAX_DEPTH more commands; one flips state to WaitingForResponse and asserts the head's get_command() text is unchanged (pre-fix fails), the other keeps state Idle and asserts the head IS drained (pins against future over-correction). Existing cmd_queue_hard_cap_drains_oldest_when_exceeded still green after the refactor.

Verification via rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-p5-orch-target:
- cargo check -p mux --lib: clean
- cargo test -p mux --lib cmd_queue: 3/3 green.

Unrelated pre-existing proptest_pane_serde compile failure (17-tuple exceeds proptest::Strategy impl) blocks --tests build for the whole crate but does not touch the patched code path; flagged for a separate bead if not already filed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-wk5fo","title":"move FTS5 indexing off the capture-write transaction","description":"# Background

Follow-up to ft-kcsae (answered). Live capture path in storage.rs
writes synchronously INTO the FTS5 index via three AFTER-triggers:

    storage.rs:169  output_segments_ai AFTER INSERT  → INSERT INTO output_segments_fts
    storage.rs:173  output_segments_ad AFTER DELETE  → fts delete
    storage.rs:177  output_segments_au AFTER UPDATE  → fts delete + insert

Every captured segment pays the inverted-index rebuild cost before
`append_segment_sync` returns (storage.rs:12308 → 12335 INSERT INTO
output_segments). Under 200-pane fanout the trigger cost is paid 200x
per-wire-tick.

# Existing primitives that can be reused

- `fts_pane_progress` table (storage.rs:472) — already tracks per-pane
  last-indexed-seq with resumable offsets.
- `sync_fts_on_startup(conn, FtsSyncConfig)` (storage.rs:16131) — the
  batched catchup engine. Currently only fires at startup.
- `FtsSyncConfig` (storage.rs:2363) — batch_size=100, max_batch_bytes=1
  MB, commit_progress per batch. Ready knobs.

# Proposal

1. Drop the three AFTER triggers on output_segments.
2. Add a writer-thread ticker (every N seconds OR after M segments
   since last catchup) that runs `sync_fts_on_startup` with the
   existing FtsSyncConfig and uses `fts_pane_progress` to pick up
   where it left off.
3. Gate behind a `storage.fts_indexing_mode = 'sync' | 'deferred'`
   config flag; default 'sync' at first, flip to 'deferred' after a
   round of search-freshness observability.
4. Expose catchup-lag (now - max(fts_pane_progress.last_indexed_at))
   as a metric so operators can spot index drift.

# Risk

- Search freshness becomes eventual (default lag = catchup interval).
- FTS rows can briefly diverge from output_segments during a crash
  between INSERT and the next catchup tick — acceptable because
  `sync_fts_on_startup` already handles gap recovery on startup.

# Measurement prerequisite

Before landing: add a bench that measures `append_segment_sync` p99
under a 200-pane load with FTS5 enabled (current) vs. triggers
disabled + deferred catchup. If the gap is not material, don't ship —
the synchronous path is simpler and the trigger tax might be in the
noise given SQLite's WAL + page cache.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"pane1","owner":"pane3-fts5-scaffold","created_at":"2026-04-21T18:00:59.588862Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:28:00.206770Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:28:00.206400Z","close_reason":"Opt-in deferred FTS indexing landed as d74401c6. Added StorageConfig.defer_fts_triggers: bool (default false, preserves current sync behavior). When true, StorageHandle::with_config executes DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS on output_segments_a[iud] immediately after initialize_schema, so new segment writes no longer synchronously rebuild FTS inverted-index pages. Callers are responsible for periodic sync_fts() invocation; the fts_pane_progress + sync_fts_on_startup engine already handles resumable batched catchup. Verified via 4 new regression tests in fts_sync_tests (17 total passing): fts_sync_mode_indexes_on_insert (baseline), fts_deferred_mode_does_not_index_on_insert (post-DROP empty FTS), fts_deferred_mode_catchup_indexes_all_segments (500 segments → catchup → 500 indexed, second catchup is no-op), fts_deferred_mode_catchup_resumes_across_rounds (5 rounds × 100 segments interleaved with catchup, each round indexes exactly 100). Surfaced but did NOT fix: pre-existing seq=0 off-by-one in sync_fts_for_pane (WHERE seq > last_indexed_seq excludes fresh pane's seq=0 segment) — tests use seq 1..=N, separate bead for that fix.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3602,"issue_id":"ft-wk5fo","author":"pane3-fts5-scaffold","text":"Claimed for scaffolding. Build verification currently blocked by ft-h9g0q (non-exhaustive match on new Error variants in error_renderer.rs:34 + retry.rs:378). Just shipped a mechanical fix in commit 87d4546b covering all 3 new typed variants; waiting on cold cargo check to confirm green. Once compile is green, scope for this bead's first shippable slice is the criterion benchmark for append_segment_sync p99 described in the ticket's 'Measurement prerequisite' — no implementation changes before we have numbers.","created_at":"2026-04-21T20:17:34Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-wn3yc","title":"[reality-gap] default config export still exposes legacy wa-prefixed paths and prefixes under ft branding","description":"The shipped binary still exports several `wa`-prefixed defaults even though the docs and product surface are branded as `ft`.

Evidence from `./FrankenTerm.app/Contents/MacOS/ft config export --json` on 2026-04-23:
- `general.data_dir` defaults to `/Users/jemanuel/Library/Application Support/wa`
- `native.socket_path` defaults to `/tmp/wa/events.sock`
- `metrics.prefix` defaults to `wa`
- `notifications.email.subject_prefix` defaults to `[wa]`

This is a separate drift from the README example block: the binary itself still carries legacy default identity in operator-visible config output.

Expected fix:
- Audit whether these are intentional compatibility defaults or stale rename residue.
- Either update the defaults to `ft`-prefixed values or document the compatibility rationale explicitly in README/docs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:34:07Z","created_by":"GreenWren","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:53:43.273929Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:53:43.273302Z","close_reason":"Completed: default config export defaults rebranded to ft in bd6b39da and 5b16f248","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["branding","docs","reality-gap"]}
-{"id":"ft-wt9bh","title":"escape-parser: OSC 8 hyperlink Display still emits unescaped separators","description":"Production path: frankenterm/escape-parser/src/hyperlink.rs Display writes OSC 8 param keys/values and URI raw. Closed bead ft-mw1nw documented this, but the code still contains the TODOs and the regression test still asserts the broken parse failure. Impact: Display can emit id=a:b or URI values with semicolons that Hyperlink::parse splits as delimiters, dropping the entire hyperlink.  {"id":"ft-wx5ln","title":"pattern pack subdirectories ignore supported rules.yaml/yml/json manifests","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:06:44.211855Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T12:10:14.601212Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T12:10:14.600868Z","close_reason":"Landed in 07e72a52: nested pack directories now discover supported rules.yaml/yml/json manifests, with regression coverage.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-wxqbt","title":"mcp_missions::mcp_save_mission_tx_contract_to_path: non-atomic write corrupts tx contract on mid-write crash","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_missions.rs:289-308 mcp_save_mission_tx_contract_to_path serializes a MissionTxContract and calls std::fs::write(path, json) directly. fs::write is NOT atomic — it open+truncate+write in a single sequence. If the process crashes (panic, SIGKILL, power loss, disk full mid-write) between truncate and final write, the target file is left truncated or with partial JSON. On next startup mcp_load_mission_tx_contract_from_path reads the partial file, serde_json::from_str bails with 'Invalid tx contract JSON', and the ENTIRE tx contract is lost — not the last update, the whole in-flight contract. Same shape applies to mcp_save_mission_contract_to_path if it exists via a copy-paste. The codebase already has the atomic-rename pattern for durable writes (config_profiles.rs:129-135 manifest writes, recorder_storage.rs:1061, rulesets.rs:182, wal_engine.rs:892). Fix: write to path.with_extension('json.tmp') first, then std::fs::rename to the final path. rename is atomic on POSIX and NTFS — the tx file is either the old contents or the new contents, never a truncated halfway state.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:05:53.858137Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T12:08:14.359806Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T12:08:14.359416Z","close_reason":"Landed in 149f35d8 (fix(mcp_missions): atomic tmp+rename for mission/tx-contract saves). Both mcp_save_mission_tx_contract_to_path and mcp_save_mission_to_path now write to <path>.json.tmp and std::fs::rename onto the target. Two regression tests (save_tx_contract_is_atomic_rename_ft_wxqbt, save_mission_is_atomic_rename_ft_wxqbt) assert target file presence + .tmp-sibling absence + round-trip parseability. Verification of the tests blocked by pre-existing frankenterm-core compile blockers (ft-kqidb/uigpj/s2u51/zv3u9).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-wze7j","title":"[review][LOW] ft-szs5k exhaustive match arm contradicts pre-assert in events_labruntime test","description":"16ed98e5 added a pre-assert AND a silent Cancelled-or-Closed match arm in tests/events_labruntime.rs:198-213. Pre-assert at :200-203 uses matches!(result, Ok or Err Lagged) and panics with 'unexpected recv result' on anything else. Match at :212 accepts Cancelled or Closed silently. If either surfaced, the pre-assert fires FIRST — the match arm is unreachable dead code. Pre-commit state was cleaner: explicit match arm that panicked with 'unexpected close'. Likely caused by compiler warning non-exhaustive after fa1fbffe added Cancelled; the match was patched to add the exhaustive arm just to silence the warning without removing the intent. Pick either: (A) remove pre-assert, restore explicit panic arms, or (B) keep pre-assert, destructure via if-let. Severity: LOW, reviewer round 12, 2026-04-23.
